commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And say to the land of Israel, Thus says the LORD; Behold, I am against you, and will draw forth my sword out of its sheath, and will cut off from you the righteous and the wicked. But had not God declared the contrary, chap. The time was not yet arrived; or, he rescued the just from death, though he permitted them to experience the other common miseries. (Calmet) He preserved them from eternal death. (Estius) The just often means those who are so only in appearance, chap. (Calmet) Temporal afflictions fall upon all; but the just derive benefit from them, while the wicked fall into eternal misery. Jerome) (Worthington).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
